tool.name: Muninn issue_close
tool.summary: Close a GitHub issue with a learning synthesis. Posts the synthesis as a closing comment, then writes it as a `decision` memory tagged with the issue number — encoded as a flowing DAG so the close ack returns the moment GitHub returns 2xx, while the memory write happens detached.
tool.description: Two-artifact close: GitHub issue gets the implementation log (the closing comment), Muninn's memory store gets the behavioral learning (the decision memory). Wraps a `flowing` graph so synthesis-text validation runs BEFORE any GitHub call fires, the close-issue ack is the terminal node (returns immediately on 2xx), and the memory-store + optional pending-test-verification run as detached side-effects whose failure populates `flow.detached_failures` rather than bubbling up. Same shape as `perch_publish` (single primary credential, structured comment) but writes to a different surface (issues, not pages) and with a paired Turso write per close. Issue #5 calls this out as the credential-reuse-with-perch_publish test.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

env.name: GH_TOKEN
env.prompt: GitHub personal access token. Needs write access (issues scope) to the target repo. Classic PAT with repo scope works; fine-grained PAT with explicit per-repo Issues:write is preferred. The same token is used by perch_publish, blog_publish, perch_triage, and verify_patch — share-by-default is intentional.

env.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
env.prompt: Optional fallback for GH_TOKEN. The source reads `os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN') or os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN')`.

env.name: TURSO_TOKEN
env.prompt: Turso libSQL auth token for the Muninn memory database. The decision memory is written here. Required.

env.name: TURSO_URL
env.prompt: Hostname of the Muninn memory libSQL database, e.g. 'mydb-username.turso.io'.

scopes.rationale: Posts a closing comment and toggles state to closed on the target issue. Reads the current issue state to decide whether the close is a no-op.
Stores the learning synthesis as a `decision`-typed memory tagged `issue-N` and the short-form repo identifier so that future recall can find it from either side of the issue/memory pair.
Talks to api.github.com (REST) for issue read/comment/close and to the configured Turso libSQL host for the memory write. No other outbound destinations.

actions.name: close
actions.summary: Validate synthesis, close the GitHub issue with it as the closing comment, and store the decision memory in the background.
actions.description: Synchronously: validates the synthesis is non-empty, posts it as a comment on the issue, closes the issue. The close-ack (issue_url + comment_url) is returned immediately. Detached: writes a `decision`-type memory with the synthesis body and tags `issue-N`, `<repo-short>`, plus any extra_tags. If pending_test=true, an additional detached node verifies the pending-test contract on the freshly-stored memory. Detached failures populate the returned `detached_failures` array; they do NOT raise. Idempotent at the GitHub layer (closing an already-closed issue returns the same shape) but NOT at the memory layer (each close stores a new memory).

docs.goal: Close a GitHub issue with a learning synthesis and persist the synthesis as a decision memory.
docs.inputs_brief: number (req), synthesis (req), repo (default oaustegard/claude-skills), pending_test (bool), extra_tags (string[])
docs.example: close number=619 synthesis='Pattern X works because Y' repo=oaustegard/claude-skills

input.synthesis: Refactor pattern X works because of Y. Constraint: Z.

transmits.purpose: post closing comment + toggle issue state

command:
import os, json, urllib.request
token = os.environ['GH_TOKEN']
req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/repos/oaustegard/claude-skills', headers={'Authorization': f'token {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'issue-close-smoke'})
d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read())
assert d['permissions']['push'], 'token lacks write access (needed for issue close)'
print('OK: write access confirmed to', d['name'])

success.stdout_regex: OK: write access confirmed to claude-skills
